note: Should-trigger / should-not-trigger queries for tuning activation. Triggers: the sigil '@@JE[:N][:M[:Z]]' (preferred) or the prose marker 'joust:N[:M[:Z]]', case-insensitive with optional spaces. N = attempts/round (optional; inferable from a prose model spec or the Top Mixed preset), M = passes (1 single, 2 two), Z = grand loops (Z=1/omitted = isolated tournament; Z>=2 = grand-loop mode: real repo writes + one PR per loop, fail-closed verify; Z_MAX=5, Z>5 refused). 'mode' is the expected mode; n/assignment/z/needsGate/conflict/error/preset are expected je-parse.mjs outputs where relevant. Non-triggers are genuine non-tournaments or other tournament phrasings that belong to the separate best-of-n skill, not this explicitly-named one.

queries.query: write a python program for hangman game, joust:4
implement a token bucket rate limiter :joust:6:2
draft a distributor pitch email, Joust:4:2
two-round attempt on this regex joust:5:2
what is iterative refinement in LLM sampling?
explain how genetic algorithms do selection and mutation
review this code and give me two passes of feedback
i made two attempts at this already, which is better?
summarise this document in two passes, skim then detail
set up a two-stage build pipeline in CI
compare Opus and Sonnet on a coding task
give me one strong solution, no tournament
what is the Joust lens in photography?

queries.comment: bare @@JE -> interactive gate

queries.query: build a parser with 2 opus, 2 glm 5.2, 1 codex high @@JE
queries.comment: headline prose spec

queries.query: do abc, 1 opus and 1 sonnet and 1 codex joust:3
queries.comment: bare codex -> codex-sol default (2026-07-14; gpt-5.6 family)
bare glm -> glm-5.2 default
minimax provider token
Top Mixed N=6 -> 2/2/2
Top Mixed leading-count N=5 -> 2/2/1
Top Mixed N=2 -> opus+glm-5.2

queries.query: improve X @@JE:4 with 2 opus, 2 glm, 1 codex
queries.comment: explicit-N-vs-prose conflict -> stop & ask

queries.query: do x with 2 opus and 1 sonnet @@JE:3
queries.comment: marker N and spec agree
grand loops now implemented: Z=3 single-pass, explicit N=5 (was the 'not yet implemented' stop)
grand loops now implemented: Z=2 two-pass (was the 'not yet implemented' stop)
positional skip forbidden; require @@JE:5:1:3
digit-noun guard: '3 bugs' is task text, n stays 5. needsGate is false (N is explicit); the SKILL still runs the Phase 1 gate because assignment is null
digit-noun guard; needsGate false (explicit N=4), assignment null so the SKILL runs the gate
'2 modules' task text ignored; '2 opus' is the spec

queries.query: do x with 2 opus and 1 gpt4 @@JE
queries.comment: unknown token rejected loudly, never dropped
no marker; ordinary digit-noun task must NOT trigger

queries.query: compare 2 opus and 1 sonnet runs side by side
queries.comment: a prose spec alone (no marker) does not trigger

queries.query: run the top mixed playlist on shuffle
queries.comment: 'top mixed' without a marker is not a tournament
grand loops: Z=3 two-pass, explicit N=4
grand loops: Z=2 single-pass
Z at the ceiling Z_MAX=5 is valid
grand loops via the prose marker
explicit Z=1 == today's behaviour, byte-identical to omitting Z

queries.query: improve X @@JE::1:3 with 2 opus, 2 glm 5.2, 1 codex high
queries.comment: N OMITTED via empty-N sigil form @@JE::1:3 (NOT @@JE:1:3); N=5 inferred from the prose spec, Z=3

queries.query: improve X @@JE:5:1:3 with 2 opus, 2 glm 5.2, 1 codex high
queries.comment: explicit N=5 that AGREES with the prose sum; Z=3
Z>Z_MAX(5) -> refuse, echo offending Z=9, ask to split into batches
fat-fingered Z=30 refused; the re-type-Z friction would also catch this

queries.query: improve X @@JE:4:1:3 with 2 opus, 2 glm, 1 codex
queries.comment: explicit-N-vs-prose conflict (4 vs 5) -> STOP and ask; Z=3 still parsed
parses fine; the SKILL's non-implementable-task detection (before loop 1) must offer Z=1 instead of opening empty/meaningless PRs

queries.query: run 3 grand loops of improvement on my repo
queries.comment: no sigil/marker; ambiguous plain request, not this skill

queries.comment: branch/PR ops, not a tournament

queries.comment: a question about the feature, not an invocation
